author: @2wheels1nut95
text: In a previous life, I had a job supervising a conveyor belt take rubble up a steep incline to a rock crusher, I remember having a similar epiphany, as the soil is taken up on the belt the soil on the edges slid down the steel sides to the bottom "ground state" This made me think about electricity and the skin heating effect, and also how in electrical thing their seems to be a sort of counter movement of electrons / current etc, for example some people struck by lightning report feeling a rush from the ground upwards shortly before being struck.

author: @dedlunch
text: Everything is in a state of angular momentum(in my eyes), from the macro to the atomic, a giant boulder sitting outside appears stationary, but the electrons in all the atoms in the boulder are in constant movement, the planet, more angular momentum, the solar system even more. Then on to galaxies, then super clusters, it the same all the way up and all the way down. I think the interfaces(boundary conditions) have their own unique properties, and that is what science looks at, without knowing what it actually is . Just my take, I thought it fit in line with what you were saying:) and thought I would share:) love your mind Rob:)

author: @wresing
text: The holographic principle says we can rewrite our theories about gravity in the bulk volume with theories on the bounding surface, just as we can transform volume integrals into surface ones. There is a similar duality between Lagrangian least action and Hamiltonian infinitesimal action. So perhaps your idea could allow for a more complete quantum gravity theory. The problem is experimental support. There is plenty of work on moving and spinning things to measure gravity fluctuations, and even rumors of positive results. The missing piece in gravity, energy, movement, and life is information.

author: @zahmac01
text:
Take a lesson from the study of "sound and music," not the movie by the way.
Sound is transferred across mediums of similar elasticities and invokes the assumption that the sound moved across a medium. Contrary to that assumption, sound is a function of impulses of alternating contraction and relaxation of the medium and does not drastically alter the position of any of the local medium on a global scale.
Characteristics of transference of sound across mediums is dependent upon the relation to gravity, the ordering of pressures and densities of the different states of matter.
The cognition of the cat must be in harmony with the state of the environment, i.e. boundary conditions and environmental conditions. Otherwise, the cat will move to a more comfortable position. 
If the sun is beating down on you, then you will be tempted to find shade, or some kind of filter to put between you and the sun. Therefore, you have altered the boundary condition to please your environment. The amount of physical effort put forth to accomplish such a task is described as movement. Conversely, mental effort can be attributed to allowing that movement to appear to be effortless, i.e. gravity.
Bearings take advantage of the effortless life form, the sphere. If let loose on a hill, the sphere would reach the bottom faster than any other shape.

replies.author: @zahmac01
replies.text:
The physical effort utilized to change the boundary condition appears to be movement.
Conversely, the mental effort utilized to change the boundary condition effects the performance of the physical effort. 
Bearings are utilized to reduce the effects of friction caused by gravity, and reduces the physical effort of the system to accomplish the task.
It is no surprise to me that the sphere is the most physically efficient and intelligent means to life.

replies.author: @zahmac01
replies.text:
Cognition is the discrete awareness of an individual and its' relation to the scope and focus of its' refined senses and abilities. 
Given the ability to create microscopes, telescopes, microphones, tactile interfaces, photodiodes, strain gauges, radiation detectors, etc..., the human senses must determine the inherent properties of these devices in order to complete the interdependent bridge of awareness between the physical and mental operations. Balancing the two operations is a must. Desiring too much physical interaction will accumulate pollution from inefficient movements. Desiring too much mental interaction will stimulate movement in directions that are perceived to be unethical, i.e. reformation/independent thinking.
It's quite possible that there is a higher life-form that is dictating the boundary conditions that cause us to initiate certain physical and mental operations. (1 of 2)

replies.author: @zahmac01
replies.text:
(2 of 2)
If you feel like you are losing your marbles by listening to me, then take a breather, grab a drink, and ponder the questions that arise in your mind. You may feel that you have the answer to those questions, and it may be so...
Now write or type those answers out so that your rational thinking may transform your abstract thought into a coherent system.
In this way, you have taken a mental abstraction and transcribed it with the help of your physical reality.
Balancing both operations allows for the creation of a new concept that is valuable to like-minded individuals.
I firmly believe that this cognitive method is a harmonious transference of sound energy between different levels, i.e. different elastic mediums.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Anthony Zahm I am sorry mate I disagree  - the way i get what you are saying is that you think movemnet arises from conditions - i disagree - conditions and movement are part of the same system and give rise to a variety of organisations of which the sphere is just one

replies.author: @zahmac01
replies.text:
+Robert Murray-Smith, thank you for the reply. I honestly don't know how to reply to this, but I will give you some more insight into what my mind is thinking and without the psychology component.
I would say that the sun's heat is an energy transfer that can either be absorbed, refracted, or reflected. A good filter would utilize the heat transfer to produce motion and/or expansion, circular motion would be exceptional for creating stability in a system.
So, yes, they are inherently the same, but any change in condition, whether it be the introduction of heat or momentum from an external system, then the conditions appear to be internalized and caused by movement of one system or another.
The paradox is that our abilities to create sensing devices is limited by our perceived reality. So, no, movement does not create or alter conditions, the mind does, which also filters energy.

author: @E.lectricityNorth
text:
I'm on that epiphany plane right now with you Robert.  I'm uncontrollably drawn to the idea of cell walls, and on a smaller scale still, electron energy levels around a nucleus.  Boundaries at which interactions take place--and at our human scale where life itself interacts, as you described with the cat slinking around the yard.  That is the kind of story that will be like Newton's Apple in the future: "Famed discoverer of the fractal electrical nature of the universe, Robert Murray-Smith, was one day hit with an epiphany about boundary interactions while watching a cat slink around the boundaries of the backyard." 

--Charge flow within a fractal electrically driven universe?  Think of the plasma boundary of the heliosphere, for a larger scale version of this type of interaction--where the solar system interacts with the galaxy surrounding it.  The boundary of the double layer in the plasma sheath around the solar system, for me, equates to a cell wall when I visualize a fractal electrical universe based on these interactions of charge between boundaries of distinct objects at all scales.  You have an amazing mind.  Thank you for sharing!

author: @gristlevonraben
text: The bbc news reported on scientists that made a tractor beam using water waves.  They found at the edges, or boundaries of the waves that a pulling force could be observed.  ( google bbc tractor beams water )  My theory on wordpress states that the offset wiggles in protons creates waves that make attractive pull forces that we call gravity.  But I have to admit, Mr. Murray, that when I was young, I got the idea for gravity as waves pulling from an accident.  We were cutting wood and the chainsaw fly wheel was horribly off balance.  It hurt our hands, but what stunned me most was when I realized the vibration was actually sucking my hand to the handle of the chainsaw.  That started me on a path that lead to my theory.  Anyway, I hope this is not a slight, but, hey, great minds think alike?  I totally agree with you about that idea.

author: @NickTonkin556
text: I don't want to start by saying I disagree, I think the observation is valid, movement is life/ life is movement, I think that one being the creator of the other is where my disagreement arises. We are compelled as "life" to think it is separate from other movement but at the core of it it is all change and the observable consumption of "life" or "energy" which result in the cessation of that change; at least for the body being considered. i.e. The Zebra dies to provide life to the Lion, the Star dies and provides matter for the Planet.

author: @penfoldooo2160
text: In ecology, the observation that biodiversity and density increases at the transition zone between two biomes (i.e. the ecotone http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ecotone) is known as an _edge effect_ (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Edge_effects).  It was deemed so important that it became the 11th Permaculture design principle (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Permaculture).  In summary, if your design increases edge, it increases the capacity for life.  The cat slinking around the transition zone between the lawn and the garden illustrates this.

author: @NeoShameMan
text: Isn't that more like gravity generate movement (by contracting space)? Too much stillness is obvious, there is no circulation of information and pattern. After all the only thing that does not change is change itself.

replies.author: @NeoShameMan
replies.text:
Robert Murray-Smith well isn't artificial gravity and gravity fundamentally different? Gravity is induce by mass deforming the space time continuous which create movement, while artificial gravity is induced acceleration onto mass by whatever force we have. That's why we walk on earth without flying out in space, because the rotation's speed is balanced by actual gravity.

But yeah That does not change the rest of the thought, we agree on "life" (or more precisely a kind of complexity) there is this concept of memetics which attribute to information life like property, and argue that life itself is pattern of informations (dna for example is the basis of all organic lifes). We can kind of conclude that we can see organisation as their own organism ... after all coca cola's culture and identity remain even though the entire staff down to the ceo change, just like we remain ourselves when all our cells are replaced by new one from matter we have digested ...
